steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage frames probabilistic models and causal determinism as seemingly incompatible, then pivots with: ‘But complete foreknowledge of relevant conditions is generally unavailable.’ The question asks which choice best states the resolution the passage implies.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — causal determinism doesn’t require absolute certainty: outcomes can be determined by conditions while remaining unpredictable from our perspective of incomplete knowledge — directly captures the resolution the pivot implies. ✓
  • Choice B — denies any reconciliation: contradicts the passage’s implied resolution. ✗
  • Choice C — emphasizes past data: shifts focus to historical evidence rather than the epistemic limitation the passage highlights. ✗
  • Choice D — imposes a requirement about overwhelming probability: adds a condition not supported by the passage. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
